sourceText: Everything is in constant flux, meaning that today is never the same as yesterday.
translatedText: Komai yana tafiya akai-akai, ma'ana yau ba kamar jiya ba.

sourceText: War is the father of all, and king of all.
translatedText: Yaki uban kowa ne, kuma sarkin kowa.

sourceText: This signifies that opposition and conflict are the fundamental forces that generate and rule all things.
translatedText: Wannan yana nuna cewa adawa da rikici su ne ginshiƙan rundunonin da ke haifar da mulkin kowane abu.

sourceText: Men do not know what they are doing when they do what they are doing, and they do not understand it when they learn.
translatedText: Maza ba su san abin da suke yi ba lokacin da suke yin abin da suke yi, kuma ba sa fahimtar sa lokacin da suka koya.

sourceText: This shows a critical insight that most people do not truly understand their actions or their consequences.
translatedText: Wannan yana nuna mahimmancin fahimta cewa yawancin mutane ba su fahimci ainihin ayyukansu ko sakamakonsu ba.

sourceText: Pythagoras faced political persecution and internal divisions within his community, forcing him to flee his homeland. He experienced the pain of his ideas being misunderstood and his community disintegrating.
translatedText: Pythagoras ya fuskanci zalunci na siyasa da rarrabuwar kawuna a cikin al'ummarsa, wanda ya tilasta masa ya gudu daga ƙasarsa. Ya dandana azabar rashin fahimtar ra'ayinsa da wargaza al'ummarsa.

sourceText: His mathematical and philosophical achievements were passed down to posterity, and his thought became a cornerstone of Western philosophy and science.
translatedText: Nasarorinsa na ilimin lissafi da na falsafa sun kasance ga zuriya, kuma tunaninsa ya zama ginshiƙin falsafa da kimiyyar Yammacin Turai.
Karni na 6-5 BC Giant (570 BC ~ 495 BC)

sourceText: The core idea of the Pythagorean school, which sought to explain the fundamental principles of the universe through numbers and ratios. It implies that all phenomena are governed by mathematical order.
translatedText: Babban ra'ayi na makarantar Pythagorean, wanda ya nemi bayyana ainihin ka'idodin sararin samaniya ta hanyar lambobi da rabo. Yana nuna cewa duk abubuwan mamaki ana sarrafa su ta tsarin lissafi.

sourceText: Pythagoras believed that the soul could be purified and virtue cultivated through the pursuit of knowledge. It emphasizes that knowing is the highest value humans should strive for.
translatedText: Pythagoras ya yi imani da cewa rai za a iya tsarkakewa da kuma tarbiyyantar da nagarta ta hanyar neman ilimi. Yana jaddada cewa sanin shine mafi girman kimar da ya kamata ɗan adam yayi ƙoƙari.

sourceText: Refrain from the practice of evil, and pursue the practice of virtue.
translatedText: Ka nisanci aikata alfasha, kuma a bi aikin kyawawan halaye.

sourceText: Beyond simply refraining from speech, this is an adage that emphasizes avoiding evil and practicing virtue. It highlights the importance of an ethical life.
translatedText: Bayan nisantar magana kawai, wannan karin magana ce da ke jaddada nisantar mugunta da aikata kyawawan halaye. Yana nuna mahimmancin rayuwar ɗabi'a.

sourceText: Man is a being who seeks truth, and knowledge is the source of all things.
translatedText: Mutum halitta ne mai neman gaskiya, kuma ilimi shi ne tushen komai.
